message: Valerian can add to the sedative effect of benzodiazepines, increasing drowsiness and CNS depression. Do not combine without medical supervision.
Combining valerian with sedative-hypnotics (e.g., zolpidem) can cause excessive sedation. Do not combine without medical supervision.
Valerian adds to opioid-related CNS depression. This combination can be dangerous; consult a clinician.
Melatonin may affect bleeding risk and interact with anticoagulants. Discuss with your prescriber before combining.
Melatonin may add to antiplatelet effects and bleeding risk. Discuss with your prescriber before combining.
Melatonin can influence blood pressure and may interact with antihypertensive medications. Monitor with your clinician.
Melatonin may affect blood glucose. If you take antidiabetic medication, monitor levels and consult your clinician.
Melatonin can stimulate immune activity and may counteract immunosuppressant therapy. Consult your prescriber.
Melatonin adds to the sedative effect of benzodiazepines. Use caution and avoid activities requiring alertness.
Magnesium can bind quinolone antibiotics and reduce their absorption. Separate doses by several hours.
Magnesium can bind tetracycline antibiotics and reduce their absorption. Separate doses by several hours.
Magnesium can reduce absorption of bisphosphonates. Separate dosing and follow your pharmacist's guidance.
With reduced kidney function, supplemental magnesium can accumulate to dangerous levels. Do not supplement without medical supervision.
L-theanine may lower blood pressure and could add to antihypertensive medication. Monitor with your clinician.
Glycine may reduce the effectiveness of clozapine. Do not combine without your prescriber's guidance.
Ashwagandha is not recommended during pregnancy. Avoid use and consult your clinician.
Ashwagandha may stimulate the immune system and counteract immunosuppressant therapy. Consult your prescriber.
Ashwagandha may increase thyroid hormone levels and add to thyroid medication. Monitor with your clinician.
Ashwagandha may add to the effect of sedative medications. Use caution and avoid activities requiring alertness.
